back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to Aaron and to his sons and to all the sons of Israel, and say to them: This is the word that the Lord has commanded, saying,
Any man from the house of Israel who slaughters an ox or a lamb or a goat in the camp, or who slaughters outside the camp—

And does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to present an offering to the Lord before the tabernacle of the Lord—blood shall be counted to that man; he has shed blood, and that man shall be cut off from among his people.

In order that the sons of Israel may bring their sacrifices that they have been sacrificing on the face of the field, and bring them to the Lord, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, to the priest, and sacrifice them as peace offerings to the Lord.
And the priest shall sprinkle the blood on the altar of the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and burn the fat as a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
And they shall no longer offer their sacrifices to the goat demons, after whom they prostitute themselves. This shall be a permanent statute for them throughout their generations.
And you shall say to them: Any man from the house of Israel, or from the foreigner who resides among them, who offers a burnt offering or a sacrifice,
And does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to make it to the Lord, that man shall be cut off from his people.

And any man from the house of Israel or from the foreigner who resides among them, who eats any blood—I will set My face against the soul that eats blood, and I will cut him off from among his people.
For the life of the flesh is in the blood, and I have given it to you upon the altar to make atonement for your souls, for the blood—it is by the life that it makes atonement.
Therefore I said to the sons of Israel: No soul among you shall eat blood, and the foreigner who resides among you shall not eat blood.
And any man from the sons of Israel or from the foreigner who resides among them, who hunts a hunt of a wild animal or bird that may be eaten—he shall pour out its blood and cover it with dirt.

For the life of all flesh is its blood; it is in its life. And I said to the sons of Israel: You shall not eat the blood of any flesh, for the life of all flesh is its blood; everyone who eats it shall be cut off.
And every soul who eats a carcass or what was torn by beasts, whether native or foreigner, shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and be unclean until evening; then he shall be clean.
But if he does not wash and bathe his flesh, then he shall bear his iniquity.
